behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This can be caught by asserting on the constructed request payload before transport, with a fake API client and no I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify() with a fixed taxonomy and a stub transport that captures outbound payload; assert the payload contains every configured label exactly once in the taxonomy field/prompt.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: A deterministic mapping rule from raw provider label to allowed taxonomy can be validated in-process with mocked API output.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a mocked API response label not present in taxonomy (e.g., "medical"); assert classify() returns "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Normalization determinism is a pure function/property check over identical inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly (or property-style loop) with the same raw response object/string and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: This specifically depends on real model/provider behavior and possible non-deterministic outputs across live calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N live calls to the provider with identical input and capture raw responses plus normalized labels; assert label consistency whenever raw provider response is unchanged, and only permit label differences when raw response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from wiring with the actual tokenizer/counting module rather than pure logic; no external API is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real tokenizer component with a document exceeding budget; call classify() up to dispatch boundary and assert request text is truncated or rejected per policy before any outbound call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: You must observe provider-reported usage from real HTTP/API responses; local mocks cannot reveal true provider accounting drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification calls for representative documents and assert provider usage fields (prompt/total tokens) are <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget report.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: To catch real retry/backoff failures you need real HTTP semantics (429/retry headers/timing), not only in-process mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable HTTP test server that returns 429 (with retry hint) then success; assert client waits per policy and eventually returns success instead of surfacing immediate error.
